name: The Content Architecture
description: A production-ready Sanity + Next.js starter with embedded Studio, catch-all routing, and schema factories.

sectionsArray.headline: The Content Architecture
sectionsArray.subheadline: A production foundation for Sanity-powered sites — schema factories, catch-all routing, cache tags, and embedded Studio.

sectionsArray.headline: What's included
sectionsArray.subheadline: Eight pillars from schema-as-system to agent-native docs.

features.title: Schema factories
features.description: Link, media, URI, page builder — reusable across every document type.

features.title: Fetch layer
features.description: CDN bypass, Data Cache tags, and webhook revalidation on publish.

features.title: Embedded Studio
features.description: One dev server, one deploy. Studio lives at /studio.

sectionsArray.headline: Three steps to a running site

steps.title: Configure env
steps.description: Copy .env.example to .env.local and add your Sanity project ID and API token.

steps.title: Import seed data
steps.description: Load the bundled homepage, site settings, and sample articles.

steps.title: Start dev
steps.description: Run the single Next.js app — site and Studio together.

sectionsArray.headline: Ready to build?
sectionsArray.subheadline: Open Studio and edit this homepage — or add new page-builder sections with Plop.

sectionsArray.headline: About this starter
sectionsArray.subheadline: This project follows The Content Architecture — decisions are made so agents and teams don't reinvent patterns on every project.

title: Welcome to The Content Architecture
excerpt: Everything you need to know about this starter — structure, env vars, and how to extend it.

children.text: This starter is a single Next.js app with embedded Sanity Studio. Pages use URI-based catch-all routing; articles live at /articles/[slug].
app/ — routes and API. features/ — Sanity client, page builder, site header/footer. sanity/ — schema factories and Studio structure.
Run pnpm sanity:typegen to regenerate TypeScript types from your schema.

seoMetadata.title: Welcome
seoMetadata.description: Getting started with The Content Architecture starter

title: Mux video setup
excerpt: How to configure Mux for video uploads in Studio.

children.text: Mux tokens are only needed in Studio for uploads — the frontend uses public playback IDs stored in Sanity documents.

seoMetadata.title: Mux video setup
seoMetadata.description: Configure Mux for Sanity Studio video uploads
